threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You order a cup of tea and ask for milk, but forget to specify 'builder's'. The barista gives you a look like you've just landed from Mars.
Caught a guy on the N37 serenading the driver with a karaoke version of 'Wonderwall' last night. The driver didn’t even flinch.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a guy on a unicycle juggling while his dog chased after him near the Richardplatz. Only in Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a techno party happening under a bridge while families picnic just a few meters away, all in total harmony.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local by how they eye the BART delays like it's a game—calculating their next move based on malfunction reports.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 31 late last night, driver chatting with a regular about how the Muni can feel like therapy. That connection? Only in SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is just about Gaudí, but they miss the quirky art studios and the local bodega where the old men gossip over café. It's the real vibe.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They’re the ones still trying to figure out the metro map, standing baffled at the entrance of every station.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look confused when the bus is late and everyone just shrugs it off. It's like a rite of passage to embrace the chaos.
Saw a couple arguing on the 35 last night, she threw his kebab out the window. Bet that’s one way to end a night out in Brixton.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I found a handwritten note on the U-Bahn seat that simply said, 'If you can read this, it’s your turn to buy coffee.' Classic Neukölln humor.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain can you find a street party breaking out at 2 PM on a Tuesday, complete with spontaneous dance-offs and free techno sets from local DJs.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s a local if they know to brace themselves before a loud bang in Civic Center—half the time it's just BART acting up again.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy juggling on the 38 Geary yesterday. He dropped a ball, but the lady next to him tossed it back without missing a beat. Classic SF moment.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think the best tapas are in the big restaurants; meanwhile, the real gems are those tiny bars hiding on side streets, serving lesser-known dishes.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always spot them by the way they order a 'café con leche' and then look bewildered when it's served in a tiny cup.
